title: GeNAS: Neural Architecture Search with Better Generalization

methodology.problem: Quantitative measurement of generalizability during architecture search is under-explored

approaches.name: ABS (Angle-Based Search)
approaches.description: Estimates angle between initial and final network parameters after convergence as a proxy performance measure
approaches.limitation: Cannot guarantee finding architectures within flat local minima

approaches.name: FBS (Flatness-Based Search)
approaches.description: Proposed method that searches for architectures with flat local minima by inspecting loss values of local neighborhood weights
approaches.advantage: Searches for architectures with flat (wide) local minima, which is considered a key signal for generalizability

previous_approaches.name: PBS (Performance-Based Search)
previous_approaches.description: Uses validation performance (accuracy or negative loss) as a proxy measure for generalizability
previous_approaches.limitation: Shows poor generalizability on test set and other tasks due to overfitting on validation set

key_findings: ABS cannot guarantee that the found architecture is located within flat local minima
FBS searches for architectures with flat local minima
Low correlation between angle measure and flatness measure on NAS-Bench-201 search space
PBS methods show large discrepancy between validation accuracy and ground truth test accuracy
empirical_observations: Architectures with fast convergence during training have a tendency to show better generalizability on test set

contributions: Proposed FBS (Flatness-Based Search) method for better generalizability in NAS
Demonstrated the importance of flat local minima in architecture generalization
Showed the limitations of ABS and PBS methods in finding generalizable architectures
